231	### Epic 1 retrospective carry-forward #2 — NOT this story
232	
233	**Story 3.3 PR atomically neutralizes `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger.** This is Story 3.3's territory, not 3.1's. `publish.yaml` stays UNTOUCHED in Story 3.1. The reason the coupling is atomic in 3.3 and not 3.1: until `release.yaml` is merged to main, `publish.yaml` is the active fallback if a contributor pushes a `v*` tag by accident. Neutralizing its trigger in 3.1 (while `release.yaml` is still on a feature branch) would break the fallback path without having replaced it.
234	
235	### Load-bearing decisions to resolve at Task 2 — rationale depth
236	
237	The five carry-forwards above have recommendations; this section captures the reasoning so the dev agent understands when deviation is appropriate.
238	
239	**(A) Why recommend staying on `conventional-changelog-cli@^5` (option a)?**
240	
241	- Story 2.2's seed used `-p conventionalcommits` preset (via `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0 → conventional-changelog@6.0.0 → conventional-changelog-conventionalcommits@8.0.0`). The seeded heading shape is `## [X.Y.Z](compare-url) (YYYY-MM-DD)`. If Story 3.1 switches to `conventional-changelog` (the upstream-suggested package) or `git-cliff`, future append entries may emit a DIFFERENT shape (dash-dated Keep-a-Changelog form, or tool-specific). That produces a CHANGELOG with mixed heading shapes across versions — an audit-trail defect.
242	- The deprecation warning is registry metadata, NOT a runtime signal. The tool continues to work. The risk is atrophy (future npm updates surface new bugs that never get fixed), not current brokenness.
243	- Epic 3 scope is "ship release-workflow reliability for v1.0.0." Swapping tools introduces revalidation work (Story 2.2 AC #1 invocation shape needs to re-verify) that bloats scope.
244	- Post-v1.0.0, a separate tool-migration story can: (i) re-seed CHANGELOG with the new tool's shape from scratch, (ii) verify the new tool's append behavior against the re-seed, (iii) rip out the deprecated dep. That is the right venue — not this one.
245	- **Deviation condition:** if Task 1's recon surfaces a runtime breakage in `conventional-changelog-cli@5` (e.g., the tool errors on the 2026-04-21 CHANGELOG.md shape, or Windows smoke in AC #16 fails), escalate to option (b) or (c) and capture the evidence in Dev Agent Record § "Completion Notes".
246	
247	**(B) Why recommend pre-inserting `[Unreleased]` anchor (option i)?**
248	
249	- Story 2.2's `-r 0` seed emitted no Unreleased heading (AC-permitted, but left the decision for Story 3.1).
250	- `conventional-changelog-cli -i CHANGELOG.md -s -r 1` behavior without an anchor is preset-specific and currently unverified (`deferred-work.md` 2-2 entry). The preset MIGHT: (a) insert above `## [0.10.0]` producing `## [next] \n ## [0.10.0]` (chronological-correct but missing the Unreleased convention), (b) skip emission because no Unreleased anchor signals "where to put the new entry," (c) emit a malformed top heading.
251	- Pre-inserting `## [Unreleased]` gives append-mode a deterministic target. Story 3.2's alpha cut then verifies the append replaces (or inserts above) that heading.
252	- Cost: one line added to CHANGELOG.md in this story. Benefit: removes an unknown from Story 3.2's failure-triage surface.
253	- **Deviation condition:** if the dev agent finds evidence (by running `conventional-changelog-cli -i ... -s -r 1` locally in a throwaway clone) that the preset handles no-anchor case cleanly, option (ii) becomes viable. Capture the evidence in Dev Agent Record § "Debug Log References".
254	
255	**(C) Why recommend bash-assertion guard (not `lychee` / `markdown-link-check`)?**
256	
257	- AC #17's scope envelope forbids `package.json` edits. Adding `lychee` or `markdown-link-check` requires a new devDep — violates scope.
258	- Bash assertion is deterministic: `! grep -E 'closes \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md`. Fails fast with a clear signal.
259	- The class of bug the guard catches is narrow: preset-misclassified commit-body tokens rendered as `closes [#<non-numeric>]`. A broader link-checker has false-positive surface (e.g., flaking on a transiently-down external URL).
260	- **Deviation condition:** if a broader link-check is deemed load-bearing later, it is a separate post-v1.0.0 story (also captured in `deferred-work.md` 2-2 entry).
261	
262	**(D) Why prose-only `.yaml` enforcement, no CI check?**
263	
264	- Story 1.3's code review Decision 1 explicitly rejected a stub file on main as enforcement overkill. The rejected reasoning applies here too: triple-guard (Dev Notes + RELEASING.md note + alpha cut as live validator) is sufficient for a low-probability, high-visibility risk.
265	- A CI check ("fail if `release.yml` exists") is a negative invariant that is hard to express cleanly and must live somewhere that enforces pre-merge.
266	- **Deviation condition:** if the reviewer misses the filename at code-review time, Story 3.2's alpha cut surfaces the 404 with a clear npm error ("npm could not match your workflow run"). Recovery: rename the file, re-run alpha cut.
267	
268	**(E) Why Windows smoke in `quality.yaml`, not `release.yaml`?**
269	
270	- `release.yaml` runs on `ubuntu-latest` (per the research doc's worked example, per `manual-release.yaml:14`). It does NOT run on Windows. So the Windows smoke can only live in a workflow that already runs on Windows — `quality.yaml` is the only one with a `windows-latest` matrix cell.
271	- `if: runner.os == 'Windows'` guard scopes the step to one matrix cell only — zero impact on Linux cell.
272	- **Deviation condition:** if the dev agent finds evidence that the Windows smoke is already implicitly covered by another existing CI step (e.g., a test:install step that exercises conventional-changelog-cli), skip this task and document. Recon in Task 1 does not currently show such coverage.

300	### Known fragility — marketplace.json sync
301	
302	AC #6 uses an unanchored sed expression inherited from `manual-release.yaml:71`:
303	
304	```bash
305	sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "X.Y.Z"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
306	```
307	
308	**This is non-global per-line but multi-line across the file.** Current marketplace.json has exactly one `"version"` field (plugin-level); sed hits that one line only. If the schema ever adds a second `"version"` field (e.g., a nested dependency version, a schema-version header), the sed replaces BOTH — silently corrupting the second field.
309	
310	Not Story 3.1's to fix. Story 5.1's pre-flight audit re-verifies marketplace.json shape before the v1.0.0 cut; if the shape has drifted, the fix is either: (a) `jq -c '.plugins[0].version = "X.Y.Z"' marketplace.json > marketplace.json.tmp && mv marketplace.json.tmp marketplace.json` (structured, resilient), or (b) anchor the sed with the plugin-level pattern. Flagged here for Story 5.1 only so the carrier knows where the fragility sits.

Story 3.2 is the first live validator — it triggers the authored workflow with `version_bump: alpha` on the feature branch, validates OIDC publish, provenance attachment, dist-tag routing, and GitHub Release creation. Splitting authoring (3.1) from live validation (3.2) is intentional: it lets the file be code-reviewed and merged-ready before any immutable alpha version number is burned.

# Story 3.1: Author canonical `release.yaml` workflow

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want a single consolidated release workflow authored at `.github/workflows/release.yaml` that replaces the ambiguous duplication across `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` + local `release:*` scripts,
so that every future release flows through one audited code path — OIDC trusted publishing with auto-provenance, dist-tag routing per channel, synced `package.json`/`marketplace.json`/`CHANGELOG.md`, reviewer-gated publish via the `release` Environment (Story 1.2), concurrency protection, and full pre-publish validation — on a feature branch ready for Story 3.2's alpha-cut validation.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **File greenfield-created** at `.github/workflows/release.yaml` on the current `feat/release-workflow` branch. The filename MUST be literal `release.yaml` (NOT `.yml`) — the npm Trusted Publisher registered 2026-04-20 binds character-for-character on `release.yaml` (see Dev Notes § "Epic 1 retro carry-forward #1 — `.yaml` vs `.yml` enforcement"). Reviewer SHALL verify filename at code-review time.

2. **Trigger & permissions** (FR2, FR4 enablement, FR12):
   - `on: workflow_dispatch` with an `inputs.version_bump` choice input with options: `alpha`, `beta`, `rc`, `patch`, `minor`, `major`.
   - `permissions: { contents: write, id-token: write }`.
   - `concurrency: { group: release, cancel-in-progress: false }`.

3. **Environment gate** (FR11): the publishing job SHALL declare `environment: release` so GitHub pauses for a required-reviewer approval before any mutation step (bump / commit / tag / push / publish). Gate references the env from Story 1.2.

4. **Setup & pre-publish validation** (NFR8, FR13):
   - `actions/checkout@v4` with `fetch-depth: 0` and `token: secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN`.
   - `actions/setup-node@v4` with `node-version-file: .nvmrc`, `cache: npm`, `registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org`.
   - `npm install -g npm@latest` step to guarantee npm CLI ≥ 11.5.1 (required for OIDC trusted-publishing mechanics).
   - `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6` with `python-version: "3.12"` (matches `quality.yaml`).
   - `npm ci`.
   - `npm test` (the full aggregate — same 12 subcommands `quality.yaml` runs).

5. **Version bump logic** (FR2 → FR9):
   - For `version_bump ∈ {alpha, beta, rc}`: `npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=<input>`.
   - For `version_bump ∈ {patch, minor, major}`: `npm version <input> --no-git-tag-version`.
   - Expose `new_version` (from `require('./package.json').version`) and `previous_tag` (from `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`) as step outputs.

6. **Multi-registry version sync** (FR8): a sync step SHALL update `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` `version` field to the bumped version via `sed` (or `jq`). Current marketplace.json has exactly one `"version"` field (top-level of the first plugin object — confirmed at recon); the unanchored sed from `manual-release.yaml:71` (`sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "..."/'`) is safe for the current shape. Reviewer SHALL re-verify this assumption at code-review time (see Dev Notes § "Known fragility — marketplace.json sync").

7. **CHANGELOG.md append** (FR6, carry-forward (B)):
   - Invoke `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` (append mode; `-s` = same-file). This is the exact invocation that the devDep added in Story 2.1 (`conventional-changelog-cli@^5.0.0`) vendors for, and that the `[Unreleased]` anchor decision from Epic 2 retro (carry-forward (B)) governs.
   - **Anchor decision at Task 2:** pick path (i) pre-insert `## [Unreleased]` line in CHANGELOG.md as a one-line edit in THIS story so append-mode has a target, or (ii) trust the preset's prepend-above-topmost-version default. Recommend (i) — see Dev Notes § "Load-bearing decisions to resolve at Task 2". Whichever path is chosen, the commit body (AC #13) MUST name it.

8. **CHANGELOG false-positive regression guard** (new from Epic 2 retro, carry-forward (C)): after the CHANGELOG append step, the workflow SHALL run a bash assertion that catches the bogus-issue-ref class of bug that Story 2.2's review caught by hand (8 × `closes [#<hex-or-anchor>]` references that the preset auto-generated from commit-body tokens and that all resolve to `/issues/404`). Recommended assertion: `! grep -E 'closes \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md` — fails the job with a clear message if any non-numeric `closes [#...]` reference appears. Runs BEFORE the commit + tag + push steps so a bad generation never produces an immutable tag. See Dev Notes § "Load-bearing decisions to resolve at Task 2".

9. **Commit + tag + push** (FR9):
   - Configure git identity: `git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"` / `git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"` (matches `manual-release.yaml:52-54`).
   - Stage `package.json`, `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, `CHANGELOG.md` (no `git add .`).
   - Commit with message exactly `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` (X.Y.Z = new_version; no emoji, no Co-Authored-By).
   - Create annotated tag `vX.Y.Z` via `git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"`. Tag creation SHALL be idempotent — skip with a log message if the tag already exists (`git rev-parse "v${{ ... }}" >/dev/null 2>&1`).
   - Push tag (`git push origin v${{ ... }}`) and push HEAD commit to `main` (`git push origin HEAD:main`).

10. **Release notes generation** (FR7):
    - Build categorized notes from `git log ${previous_tag}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse` with grep sections: `### New Features` (feat|Feature), `### Bug Fixes` (fix|Fix), `### Other Changes` (everything except feat/fix/chore/release:), `### Maintenance` (chore|Chore).
    - Notes SHALL include a fenced `## Installation` block: `` ```bash\nnpx bmad-module-skill-forge install\n``` ``.
    - Notes SHALL include `**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/${previous_tag}...v${new_version}`.
    - Output via `GITHUB_OUTPUT` heredoc using the `RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF … EOF` pattern.

11. **OIDC publish** (FR3, FR4, FR5):
    - Publish step env: `NPM_TOKEN: ""` (explicit empty — defense-in-depth per research doc line 826; forces OIDC, never falls back to a stale token).
    - Dist-tag routing: `alpha` → `--tag alpha`; `beta` → `--tag beta`; `rc` → `--tag rc`; otherwise `--tag latest`. Match on the bumped version string (`[[ "$VERSION" == *"<chan>"* ]]`) per `manual-release.yaml:154-164` pattern, extended with `rc`.
    - NO `--provenance` flag (automatic under OIDC — SLSA Build Level 2).

12. **GitHub Release** (FR10):
    - `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`.
    - `tag_name: v${new_version}`, `name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${new_version}"`.
    - `body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}`.
    - `prerelease: true` when `new_version` contains any of `alpha|beta|rc`; `false` otherwise.
    - `draft: false`.

13. **Step summary** (FR20): `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` SHALL contain: a header `## 🎉 Released v<version>`, npm URL (`https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/<version>`), GitHub release URL, and one line confirming automatic provenance attachment.

14. **Commit hygiene** (AC #9 carry-forward from Story 2.x pattern): PR / commit body on `feat/release-workflow` SHALL reference FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4, FR5, FR6, FR7, FR8, FR9, FR10, FR11 (workflow-side), FR12, FR13, FR20. Body MUST also record: (i) the `[Unreleased]` anchor decision from AC #7 and rationale, (ii) the tooling decision from carry-forward (A) — stay on `conventional-changelog-cli@^5` vs migrate vs switch — and rationale, (iii) one-line confirmation the file has been verified as `release.yaml` (literal, not `.yml`), (iv) one-line restatement that no `package.json` / workflow-other-than-release.yaml / source changes piggybacked in this commit, (v) confirmation that the workflow has NOT been merged to main (Story 3.3's scope).

15. **Do NOT merge to main in this story.** The workflow MUST be created on `feat/release-workflow` only. Story 3.2 validates via alpha cut from the feature branch; Story 3.3 owns the main-merge plus atomic `publish.yaml` tag-trigger neutralization (Epic 1 retro carry-forward #2).

16. **Windows-cell smoke step for `conventional-changelog-cli` bin resolution** (carry-forward (E)): in the SAME commit, add an `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help` step to `quality.yaml`'s windows-latest matrix cell in the `validate` job (between `Install dependencies` and `Validate YAML schemas`). This surfaces any `tempfile@5`-class Windows path/EOL breakage BEFORE Story 3.2's alpha cut relies on the tool in `release.yaml`. If the smoke fails on Windows, HALT and reconsider the tooling decision from carry-forward (A).

17. **Scope envelope — no file outside this list SHALL be modified:**
    - `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (new, ~100-130 lines).
    - `.github/workflows/quality.yaml` (+ ~3 lines for AC #16 Windows smoke step).
    - `CHANGELOG.md` (+ 1-2 lines if AC #7 path (i) is chosen — the `## [Unreleased]` anchor).
    - No changes to `package.json` (no new scripts — Story 4.2/6.2 own the `release:*` cleanup), `package-lock.json`, `publish.yaml` / `manual-release.yaml` (Story 3.3 and Epic 6 territory), `docs/RELEASING.md` (Story 4.1 owns the rollback playbook expansion), `docs/STABILITY.md` (Story 5.1 owns the lock), `README.md`, `tools/**`, `src/**`, `test/**`.

18. **Full quality suite passes on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD after commit.** `npm test` SHALL remain green (the only file-level additions are `.github/workflows/*` — outside the Prettier + ESLint + markdownlint surface for `**/*.{js,cjs,mjs,json,yaml}` formatted via `prettier --check`). YAML schema validation (`npm run validate:schemas`) SHALL pass on the new workflow file. Reviewer SHALL verify no pre-existing green check drifted to red as a side effect.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] **Task 1 — Live-state recon** (Epic 1 retrospective carry-forward — always open a story with a live-state recon block; Story 2.2 + 2.3 Task 1 set the pattern)
  - [ ] `ls .github/workflows/release.yaml 2>&1` — confirm absent (recon snapshot 2026-04-21 shows greenfield). If the file exists, STOP and reconcile out-of-band
  - [ ] `ls .github/workflows/publish.yaml .github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` — confirm both present (40 + 188 lines respectively); these are the reference implementations Story 3.1 consolidates from
  - [ ] `cat .nvmrc` — confirm `22`; `jq '.engines' package.json` — confirm `{"node": ">=22.0.0"}`
  - [ ] `jq '.devDependencies["conventional-changelog-cli"]' package.json` — confirm `"^5.0.0"` (Story 2.1 pin); `npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --version` — confirm `5.0.0` resolves
  - [ ] `head -5 CHANGELOG.md` — confirm seeded shape: `# Changelog`, Keep-a-Changelog preamble, topmost section is `## [0.10.0](...)` (preset-emitted, NOT literal Keep-a-Changelog dash-dated form); **confirm NO `## [Unreleased]` anchor present** (feeds AC #7 decision)
  - [ ] `jq '.plugins[0] | {name, version}' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — confirm single `"version"` field at plugin-level; `grep -cE '"version":\s*"[^"]*"' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — verify count = 1 (if > 1, the unanchored sed from `manual-release.yaml:71` would hit multiple lines — re-assess AC #6)
  - [ ] `gh api /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release` — confirm environment exists with `protection_rules` containing a required-reviewer; confirm env has zero secrets (`gh api /repos/.../environments/release/secrets` → `total_count: 0`)
  - [ ] Re-read `deferred-work.md § "Story 3.1 tooling decision"` for carry-forward (A) inputs. Re-read `deferred-work.md § "[Unreleased] anchor decision for Story 3.1 append mode"` for carry-forward (B) inputs. Re-read Epic 1 retro § "Carry-forward into Epic 3 #1" for the `.yaml`-enforcement flag

- [ ] **Task 2 — Resolve the five load-bearing decisions from carry-forwards (A)–(E)** (AC #1, #7, #8, #16)
  - [ ] **(A) Tooling decision** — three options: (a) stay on `conventional-changelog-cli@^5` [RECOMMENDED], (b) migrate to the upstream-suggested `conventional-changelog` package, (c) switch to `git-cliff`. **Recommendation (a):** Story 2.2's seed used the `-p conventionalcommits` preset; switching tools now would mean future append entries emit a different shape than the seeded file (mixed heading forms in one CHANGELOG.md); the deprecation is registry-side noise, not runtime breakage; Epic 3 scope is release-workflow reliability, not tooling migration. Post-v1.0.0, a separate tool-migration story is the right venue if atrophy proves real. Capture the chosen option + rationale in the commit body (AC #14 (ii))
  - [ ] **(B) `[Unreleased]` anchor** — two options: (i) pre-insert `## [Unreleased]` as a one-line CHANGELOG.md edit in THIS story [RECOMMENDED], (ii) trust preset's prepend-above-topmost-version default. **Recommendation (i):** explicit anchor is testable (Story 3.2 alpha cut verifies the append inserts above the `[Unreleased]` heading and below the `# Changelog` top-level heading), costs one line added to CHANGELOG.md, removes ambiguity from Story 3.2 failure diagnosis. If path (ii) is taken, the alpha cut may produce a malformed top heading or refuse — preset-specific behavior that is currently unverified per `deferred-work.md`. Capture in commit body (AC #14 (i))
  - [ ] **(C) Bogus-issue-ref guard** — bash-assertion approach, AC #8 as written (`! grep -E 'closes \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md`). Alternative: `lychee` or `markdown-link-check` as a new devDep — heavier, changes scope envelope (AC #17 forbids `package.json` edits). Stick with bash-assertion; revisit at a post-v1.0.0 hardening pass
  - [ ] **(D) `.yaml` vs `.yml` literal filename** — prose-only enforcement (Epic 1 retro carry-forward #1). Reviewer must visually check the file path at code-review time. No CI check is added in this story (adding one was explicitly rejected as overkill in Story 1.3's code review — the triple-guard is: Story 3.1 Dev Notes, `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` rename-coupling note, Story 3.2 alpha cut as live validator)
  - [ ] **(E) Windows-cell smoke for `conventional-changelog-cli`** — add the smoke step per AC #16. Single-line addition. If the smoke test surfaces a Windows-only path/EOL breakage, HALT Story 3.1 and reconsider carry-forward (A)'s decision (tooling migration may become non-optional)

- [ ] **Task 3 — Author `.github/workflows/release.yaml`** (AC #1–#13)
  - [ ] Create the file greenfield. Base shape: copy the structure from `manual-release.yaml` (which is 90% of what we need), then apply the following deltas:
    - Add `rc` to the `version_bump` choice options (currently only `alpha|beta|patch|minor|major`)
    - Add `permissions: { id-token: write, contents: write }` at workflow level (currently only `contents: write, packages: write` — drop `packages: write`, add `id-token: write`)
    - Add `concurrency: { group: release, cancel-in-progress: false }` at workflow level (currently absent)

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

This is a **consolidation, not a rewrite**. `manual-release.yaml` (188 lines) already encodes ~90% of what Story 3.1 produces — version bump logic, marketplace.json sync, release-notes generation, git config, tag idempotency check, GitHub Release creation, summary emit. The Epic 3 scope delta is narrow and surgical:

- **Add** OIDC scaffolding (`permissions.id-token`, `NPM_TOKEN: ""`, drop `--provenance` flag, drop `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` env on publish step).
- **Add** the Environment gate (`environment: release`).
- **Add** concurrency protection.
- **Add** latest-npm CLI install (OIDC floor).
- **Add** CHANGELOG append step + bogus-ref assertion.
- **Add** `rc` channel.
- **Replace** `git add .` with explicit three-file staging.
- **Keep** most interior patterns verbatim (git config, sed shape, tag idempotency, release-notes grep categorization).

Target file size: ~100-130 lines. The research doc's worked example (lines 688-853, 165 lines counting the comment block and blank lines) is the closest shape to what will ship. `manual-release.yaml` is the secondary reference for interior details the research doc leaves implicit.

The file is authored **on `feat/release-workflow` only**. Do NOT merge to `main` — that is Story 3.3's scope and is coupled with an atomic neutralization of `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger (Epic 1 retro carry-forward #2 / `deferred-work.md § "Story 3.3 precondition"`). Merging Story 3.1 alone leaves a window where both workflows can fire on the same tag push.

**Scope discipline — what NOT to do here:**

- Do NOT delete `publish.yaml` or `manual-release.yaml`. Story 3.3 retains them as inert backup; Epic 6 Story 6.1 deletes them post-v1.0.0. AC #17 enforces.
- Do NOT remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`. Story 4.2 adds deprecation prefixes; Story 6.2 deletes them. AC #17 enforces.
- Do NOT extend `docs/RELEASING.md` with rollback playbook content. Story 4.1 owns the six-scenario rollback playbook; the `## Rollback Playbook` heading in RELEASING.md is an intentional placeholder (Epic 1 retro carry-forward).
- Do NOT flip the `prevent_self_review` env setting. That is a standing flag from Epic 1 retro pending a second reviewer onboarding; no fire-date.
- Do NOT add `NPM_TOKEN` to the `release` environment. Story 1.2 established the invariant: `release` env has zero secrets. AC #17 keeps env untouched; the `NPM_TOKEN: ""` in AC #11 is a step-env override, not an environment-scoped secret.
- Do NOT switch off the conventional-changelog-cli tooling. Carry-forward (A) recommends staying on `@^5` for v1.0.0 — switching invalidates Story 2.2's seed consistency.
- Do NOT trigger the workflow in this story. The workflow is authored for Story 3.2 to validate via `workflow_dispatch` with `version_bump: alpha`. Triggering from Story 3.1 pre-merges a test cut that should be Story 3.2's bench.
- Do NOT sign the tag with `git tag -s` or Sigstore Gitsign. The research doc § "Optional stretch" lists Sigstore as nice-to-have for v1.0.0 specifically, not for every release; adding it to `release.yaml` commits every future release to the signing path. Out of scope for 3.1; revisit at Story 5.1 pre-flight for a v1.0.0-specific one-time signed tag.
- Do NOT add `npm sbom`, `dependency-review-action`, or any other stretch-goal item from the research doc § "Stretch / optional". These are post-v1.0.0 hardening — each adds surface that Story 5.1 would have to audit.
- Do NOT author a throwaway validation workflow to "prove OIDC works" separately. Story 3.2 is the live validator; authoring a second workflow here violates Epic 1's scope-discipline pattern and creates a surface future stories would have to retire.

### Epic 1 retrospective carry-forward #2 — NOT this story

**Story 3.3 PR atomically neutralizes `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger.** This is Story 3.3's territory, not 3.1's. `publish.yaml` stays UNTOUCHED in Story 3.1. The reason the coupling is atomic in 3.3 and not 3.1: until `release.yaml` is merged to main, `publish.yaml` is the active fallback if a contributor pushes a `v*` tag by accident. Neutralizing its trigger in 3.1 (while `release.yaml` is still on a feature branch) would break the fallback path without having replaced it.

### Load-bearing decisions to resolve at Task 2 — rationale depth

The five carry-forwards above have recommendations; this section captures the reasoning so the dev agent understands when deviation is appropriate.

**(A) Why recommend staying on `conventional-changelog-cli@^5` (option a)?**

- Story 2.2's seed used `-p conventionalcommits` preset (via `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0 → conventional-changelog@6.0.0 → conventional-changelog-conventionalcommits@8.0.0`). The seeded heading shape is `## [X.Y.Z](compare-url) (YYYY-MM-DD)`. If Story 3.1 switches to `conventional-changelog` (the upstream-suggested package) or `git-cliff`, future append entries may emit a DIFFERENT shape (dash-dated Keep-a-Changelog form, or tool-specific). That produces a CHANGELOG with mixed heading shapes across versions — an audit-trail defect.
- The deprecation warning is registry metadata, NOT a runtime signal. The tool continues to work. The risk is atrophy (future npm updates surface new bugs that never get fixed), not current brokenness.
- Epic 3 scope is "ship release-workflow reliability for v1.0.0." Swapping tools introduces revalidation work (Story 2.2 AC #1 invocation shape needs to re-verify) that bloats scope.
- Post-v1.0.0, a separate tool-migration story can: (i) re-seed CHANGELOG with the new tool's shape from scratch, (ii) verify the new tool's append behavior against the re-seed, (iii) rip out the deprecated dep. That is the right venue — not this one.
- **Deviation condition:** if Task 1's recon surfaces a runtime breakage in `conventional-changelog-cli@5` (e.g., the tool errors on the 2026-04-21 CHANGELOG.md shape, or Windows smoke in AC #16 fails), escalate to option (b) or (c) and capture the evidence in Dev Agent Record § "Completion Notes".

**(B) Why recommend pre-inserting `[Unreleased]` anchor (option i)?**

- Story 2.2's `-r 0` seed emitted no Unreleased heading (AC-permitted, but left the decision for Story 3.1).
- `conventional-changelog-cli -i CHANGELOG.md -s -r 1` behavior without an anchor is preset-specific and currently unverified (`deferred-work.md` 2-2 entry). The preset MIGHT: (a) insert above `## [0.10.0]` producing `## [next] \n ## [0.10.0]` (chronological-correct but missing the Unreleased convention), (b) skip emission because no Unreleased anchor signals "where to put the new entry," (c) emit a malformed top heading.
- Pre-inserting `## [Unreleased]` gives append-mode a deterministic target. Story 3.2's alpha cut then verifies the append replaces (or inserts above) that heading.
- Cost: one line added to CHANGELOG.md in this story. Benefit: removes an unknown from Story 3.2's failure-triage surface.
- **Deviation condition:** if the dev agent finds evidence (by running `conventional-changelog-cli -i ... -s -r 1` locally in a throwaway clone) that the preset handles no-anchor case cleanly, option (ii) becomes viable. Capture the evidence in Dev Agent Record § "Debug Log References".

**(C) Why recommend bash-assertion guard (not `lychee` / `markdown-link-check`)?**

- AC #17's scope envelope forbids `package.json` edits. Adding `lychee` or `markdown-link-check` requires a new devDep — violates scope.
- Bash assertion is deterministic: `! grep -E 'closes \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md`. Fails fast with a clear signal.
- The class of bug the guard catches is narrow: preset-misclassified commit-body tokens rendered as `closes [#<non-numeric>]`. A broader link-checker has false-positive surface (e.g., flaking on a transiently-down external URL).
- **Deviation condition:** if a broader link-check is deemed load-bearing later, it is a separate post-v1.0.0 story (also captured in `deferred-work.md` 2-2 entry).

**(D) Why prose-only `.yaml` enforcement, no CI check?**

- Story 1.3's code review Decision 1 explicitly rejected a stub file on main as enforcement overkill. The rejected reasoning applies here too: triple-guard (Dev Notes + RELEASING.md note + alpha cut as live validator) is sufficient for a low-probability, high-visibility risk.
- A CI check ("fail if `release.yml` exists") is a negative invariant that is hard to express cleanly and must live somewhere that enforces pre-merge.
- **Deviation condition:** if the reviewer misses the filename at code-review time, Story 3.2's alpha cut surfaces the 404 with a clear npm error ("npm could not match your workflow run"). Recovery: rename the file, re-run alpha cut.

**(E) Why Windows smoke in `quality.yaml`, not `release.yaml`?**

- `release.yaml` runs on `ubuntu-latest` (per the research doc's worked example, per `manual-release.yaml:14`). It does NOT run on Windows. So the Windows smoke can only live in a workflow that already runs on Windows — `quality.yaml` is the only one with a `windows-latest` matrix cell.
- `if: runner.os == 'Windows'` guard scopes the step to one matrix cell only — zero impact on Linux cell.
- **Deviation condition:** if the dev agent finds evidence that the Windows smoke is already implicitly covered by another existing CI step (e.g., a test:install step that exercises conventional-changelog-cli), skip this task and document. Recon in Task 1 does not currently show such coverage.

### Known fragility — marketplace.json sync

AC #6 uses an unanchored sed expression inherited from `manual-release.yaml:71`:

```bash
sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "X.Y.Z"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
```

**This is non-global per-line but multi-line across the file.** Current marketplace.json has exactly one `"version"` field (plugin-level); sed hits that one line only. If the schema ever adds a second `"version"` field (e.g., a nested dependency version, a schema-version header), the sed replaces BOTH — silently corrupting the second field.

Not Story 3.1's to fix. Story 5.1's pre-flight audit re-verifies marketplace.json shape before the v1.0.0 cut; if the shape has drifted, the fix is either: (a) `jq -c '.plugins[0].version = "X.Y.Z"' marketplace.json > marketplace.json.tmp && mv marketplace.json.tmp marketplace.json` (structured, resilient), or (b) anchor the sed with the plugin-level pattern. Flagged here for Story 5.1 only so the carrier knows where the fragility sits.

5. **Scope envelope** — `git diff --cached --stat` shows only the expected 3-4 files (Task 7).
6. **`.yaml` filename** — reviewer visual check at code-review time (AC #1 + Task 6 grep check).

**Why no live validation of the workflow in this story?** Because that is Story 3.2's scope. Story 3.2 is the first live validator — it triggers the authored workflow with `version_bump: alpha` on the feature branch, validates OIDC publish, provenance attachment, dist-tag routing, and GitHub Release creation. Splitting authoring (3.1) from live validation (3.2) is intentional: it lets the file be code-reviewed and merged-ready before any immutable alpha version number is burned.

### Project Structure Notes

**Files modified by this story (entire repo diff):**

- `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — NEW (~100-130 lines, structured per the research doc's worked example + the delta list above).
- `.github/workflows/quality.yaml` — MODIFIED (+~3 lines: one new step with `if: runner.os == 'Windows'` guard in the `validate` job).
- `CHANGELOG.md` — MODIFIED (+~2 lines: `## [Unreleased]` anchor + trailing blank line) — ONLY IF Task 2 chose path (i).

**Files NOT modified (and the rationale for each):**

- `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` — Story 3.3 territory (neutralize tag trigger + comment header in same PR); must stay untouched here so the fallback path remains intact until `release.yaml` is on `main`.
- `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` — Story 3.3 adds deprecation comment header; Story 6.1 deletes. Reference only for this story.
- `package.json` / `package-lock.json` — no new deps, no new scripts. Story 2.1 vendored the tool; Story 3.1 only USES it in workflow YAML.
- `docs/RELEASING.md` — Story 4.1's territory (six-scenario rollback playbook fills the `## Rollback Playbook` placeholder).
- `docs/STABILITY.md` — Story 5.1's territory (DRAFT → STABLE flip).
- `README.md` — no new sections. The existing `## Release Process` section already references `docs/RELEASING.md`.
- `tools/**`, `src/**`, `test/**` — out of scope for a workflow-only story.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — Story 4.2 adds the "canonical release path" section.

**No new directories created.** `.github/workflows/` already exists.

content.content:
1	name: Manual Release
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_dispatch:
5	    inputs:
6	      version_bump:
7	        description: Version bump type
8	        required: true
9	        default: alpha
10	        type: choice
11	        options:
12	          - alpha
13	          - beta
14	          - patch
15	          - minor
16	          - major
17	
18	permissions:
19	  contents: write
20	  packages: write
21	
22	jobs:
23	  release:
24	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
25	    steps:
26	      - name: Checkout
27	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
28	        with:
29	          fetch-depth: 0
30	          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
31

file.content:
name: Manual Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write
  packages: write

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: |
          npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)          npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add .
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          # Get commits since last tag
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)

          # Categorize commits
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)  
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          # Build release notes
          echo "## What's New" > release_notes.md
          echo "" >> release_notes.md

          if [ -n "$FEATURES" ]; then
            echo "### New Features" >> release_notes.md
            echo "$FEATURES" >> release_notes.md
            echo "" >> release_notes.md
          fi

          if [ -n "$FIXES" ]; then
            echo "### Bug Fixes" >> release_notes.md
            echo "$FIXES" >> release_notes.md
            echo "" >> release_notes.md
          fi

          if [ -n "$OTHERS" ]; then
            echo "### Other Changes" >> release_notes.md
            echo "$OTHERS" >> release_notes.md
            echo "" >> release_notes.md
          fi

          if [ -n "$CHORES" ]; then
            echo "### Maintenance" >> release_notes.md
            echo "$CHORES" >> release_notes.md
            echo "" >> release_notes.md
          fi

          echo "## Installation" >> release_notes.md
          echo "" >> release_notes.md
          echo '```bash' >> release_notes.md
          echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install" >> release_notes.md
          echo '```' >> release_notes.md
          echo "" >> release_notes.md
          echo "**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >> release_notes.md

          # Output for GitHub Actions
          echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          cat release_notes.md >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          # Check if tag already exists
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push changes to main
        run: |
          if git push origin HEAD:main 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "✅ Successfully pushed to main branch"
          else
            echo "⚠️ Could not push to main (protected branch). This is expected."
            echo "📝 Version bump and tag were created successfully."
          fi

      - name: Publish to NPM
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then
            echo "Publishing alpha prerelease with --tag alpha"
            npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"* ]]; then
            echo "Publishing beta prerelease with --tag beta"
            npm publish --tag beta
          else
            echo "Publishing stable version with --tag latest"
            npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: |
            ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "## 🎉 Successfully released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}!" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### 📦 Distribution" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- **NPM**: Published with @latest tag" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "- **GitHub Release**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "### ✅ Installation" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "\`\`\`bash" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge@${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} install" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "\`\`\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

file.content:
name: Quality & Validation

# Runs comprehensive quality checks on all PRs:
# - Prettier (formatting)
# - ESLint (linting)
# - markdownlint (markdown quality)
# - Schema validation (YAML structure)
# - Agent schema tests (fixture-based validation)
# - Installation component tests (CLI + compilation)
# - Workflow state consistency tests
# - Knowledge base tests
# - Skill structure validation
# - File reference validation
# - Python script tests (pytest via uv)

"on":
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  prettier:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Prettier format check
        run: npm run format:check

  eslint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: ESLint
        run: npm run lint

  markdownlint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: markdownlint
        run: npm run lint:md

  validate:
    # Matrix Linux + Windows to catch platform-divergence bugs (path handling,
    # line endings, shell scripts). macOS skipped: POSIX-equivalent to Linux
    # for SKF's Node + Python surface area — add only if a Mac-only bug surfaces.
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Validate YAML schemas
        run: npm run validate:schemas

      - name: Run agent schema validation tests
        run: npm run test:schemas

      - name: Test installation components
        run: npm run test:install

      - name: Test CLI integration
        run: npm run test:cli

      - name: Test workflow state consistency
        run: npm run test:workflow

      - name: Test knowledge base
        run: npm run test:knowledge

      - name: Validate skills structure
        run: npm run validate:skills

      - name: Validate file references
        run: npm run validate:refs

  python:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run Python tests
        run: npm run test:python

content.subject: Task 5 — Add Windows smoke step to quality.yaml
content.description: Add conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution smoke test step in validate job, gated with if: runner.os == 'Windows'. Format-check.

content.subject: Task 3 — Author .github/workflows/release.yaml
content.description: Create the greenfield release.yaml with OIDC scaffolding, environment:release, concurrency, rc channel, CHANGELOG append, bogus-ref assertion, dist-tag routing, softprops release, expanded summary. ~100-130 lines.

content.subject: Task 7 — Stage + commit
content.description: Confirm diff shape matches AC #17 scope envelope (≤ 3 files: release.yaml, quality.yaml, CHANGELOG.md). Commit with conventional subject and AC #14 body (5 required elements).

content.subject: Task 4 — Pre-insert [Unreleased] anchor in CHANGELOG.md
content.description: Insert ## [Unreleased] heading + blank line after Keep-a-Changelog preamble, above ## [0.10.0]. Lint-check.

content.subject: Task 1 — Live-state recon
content.description: Verify release.yaml absent; publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml present; .nvmrc=22; engines.node; conventional-changelog-cli devDep @^5; CHANGELOG shape (no [Unreleased]); marketplace.json single version field; release env exists with reviewer and zero secrets

content.subject: Task 2 — Resolve load-bearing decisions (A)-(E)
content.description: Record resolution for five carry-forwards: (A) tooling stay on cc-cli@5, (B) pre-insert Unreleased anchor, (C) bash grep guard, (D) prose-only .yaml enforcement, (E) Windows smoke in quality.yaml

content.subject: Task 6 — Full quality suite regression gate
content.description: Run npm test; ensure format:check, lint, lint:md, validate:schemas, validate:refs all pass. Grep docs for release.yaml/.yml literal consistency.

name: Publish to NPM

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write # Required for OIDC

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run full test suite
        run: npm test

      - name: Publish to NPM
        run: npm publish --provenance
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

content.content:
680	_Source:_ [azu/setup-npm-trusted-publish](https://github.com/azu/setup-npm-trusted-publish)
681	
682	### Worked Example: Recommended SKF v1.0.0 Release Workflow
683	
684	Below is the canonical workflow that consolidates `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` into one file, migrates to OIDC trusted publishing, adds environment protection, fixes the concurrency gap, and supports `rc`/`next` channels alongside the existing `alpha`/`beta`.
685	
686	**File**: `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (replaces both `publish.yaml` and `manual-release.yaml`)
687	
688	```yaml
689	name: Release
690	
691	on:
692	  workflow_dispatch:
693	    inputs:
694	      version_bump:
695	        description: Version bump type
696	        required: true
697	        default: alpha
698	        type: choice
699	        options:
700	          - alpha
701	          - beta
702	          - rc
703	          - patch
704	          - minor
705	          - major
706	
707	permissions:
708	  contents: write   # tag + commit push
709	  id-token: write   # OIDC for trusted publishing
710	
711	concurrency:
712	  group: release
713	  cancel-in-progress: false   # let in-flight release finish; refuse double-trigger
714	
715	jobs:
716	  release:
717	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
718	    environment: release   # gates on environment protection rule (required reviewer)
719	    steps:
720	      - name: Checkout
721	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
722	        with:
723	          fetch-depth: 0
724	          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
725	
726	      - name: Setup Node.js
727	        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
728	        with:
729	          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
730	          cache: npm
731	          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org
732	
733	      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
734	        run: npm install -g npm@latest   # require npm >= 11.5.1
735	
736	      - name: Install uv
737	        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
738	        with:
739	          python-version: "3.12"
740	
741	      - name: Install dependencies
742	        run: npm ci
743	
744	      - name: Run tests and validation
745	        run: npm test
746	
747	      - name: Configure Git
748	        run: |
749	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
750	          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
751	
752	      - name: Bump version
753	        run: |
754	          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
755	            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
756	            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
757	          esac
758	
759	      - name: Get new version and previous tag
760	        id: version
761	        run: |
762	          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
763	          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
764	
765	      - name: Update marketplace.json version
766	        run: |
767	          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
768	
769	      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
770	        run: |
771	          # Generates Keep-a-Changelog entry for the new version from conventional commits.
772	          # Requires conventional-changelog-cli (added as devDependency) — or replace with git-cliff.
773	          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s
774	
775	      - name: Commit version bump
776	        run: |
777	          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
778	          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
779	
780	      - name: Generate release notes
781	        id: release_notes
782	        run: |
783	          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
784	          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
785	          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
786	          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
787	          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)
788	
789	          {
790	            echo "## What's New"
791	            echo
792	            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
793	            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
794	            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
795	            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
796	            echo "## Installation"
797	            echo
798	            echo '```bash'
799	            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
800	            echo '```'
801	            echo
802	            echo "**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
803	          } > release_notes.md
804	
805	          {
806	            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
807	            cat release_notes.md
808	            echo "EOF"
809	          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
810	
811	      - name: Create and sign tag
812	        run: |
813	          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
814	            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping"
815	          else
816	            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
817	            # Optional: switch to `git tag -s` if Sigstore Gitsign is configured in the runner
818	            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
819	          fi
820	
821	      - name: Push commit to main
822	        run: git push origin HEAD:main
823	
824	      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
825	        env:
826	          NPM_TOKEN: ""   # explicit empty: force OIDC, never fall back to a stale token
827	        run: |
828	          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
829	          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
830	          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
831	          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
832	          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
833	          fi
834	
835	      - name: Create GitHub Release
836	        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
837	        with:
838	          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
839	          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
840	          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
841	          draft: false
842	          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}
843	
844	      - name: Summary
845	        run: |
846	          {
847	            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
848	            echo
849	            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
850	            echo "**GitHub**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
851	            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
852	          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
853	```
854	
855	**What's removed vs current state**:
856	- `publish.yaml` (deleted; functionality folded in)
857	- `release:*` npm scripts in `package.json` (deleted; not needed if releases go through CI)
858	- Local manual flow (deprecated; documented in CONTRIBUTING.md as "do not use")
859	
860	**What's added**:
861	- `concurrency:` block (prevents double-run)
862	- `environment: release` (manual approval gate via env protection rule)
863	- `npm install -g npm@latest` (ensures CLI ≥ 11.5.1 for OIDC support)
864	- `rc` channel support
865	- `CHANGELOG.md` generation step (Keep-a-Changelog format from conventional commits)
866	- `NPM_TOKEN: ""` (defense-in-depth for OIDC)
867	- No more `--provenance` flag (automatic with OIDC)
868	
869	**Confidence**: HIGH — every line is grounded in cited current docs. Only caveat: requires npmjs.com side configuration (next section).

file.content:
_Source:_ [azu/setup-npm-trusted-publish](https://github.com/azu/setup-npm-trusted-publish)

### Worked Example: Recommended SKF v1.0.0 Release Workflow

Below is the canonical workflow that consolidates `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` into one file, migrates to OIDC trusted publishing, adds environment protection, fixes the concurrency gap, and supports `rc`/`next` channels alongside the existing `alpha`/`beta`.

**File**: `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (replaces both `publish.yaml` and `manual-release.yaml`)

```yaml
name: Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write   # tag + commit push
  id-token: write   # OIDC for trusted publishing

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false   # let in-flight release finish; refuse double-trigger

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release   # gates on environment protection rule (required reviewer)
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest   # require npm >= 11.5.1

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          # Generates Keep-a-Changelog entry for the new version from conventional commits.
          # Requires conventional-changelog-cli (added as devDependency) — or replace with git-cliff.
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and sign tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            # Optional: switch to `git tag -s` if Sigstore Gitsign is configured in the runner
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""   # explicit empty: force OIDC, never fall back to a stale token
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
```

**What's removed vs current state**:
- `publish.yaml` (deleted; functionality folded in)
- `release:*` npm scripts in `package.json` (deleted; not needed if releases go through CI)
- Local manual flow (deprecated; documented in CONTRIBUTING.md as "do not use")

**What's added**:
- `concurrency:` block (prevents double-run)
- `environment: release` (manual approval gate via env protection rule)
- `npm install -g npm@latest` (ensures CLI ≥ 11.5.1 for OIDC support)
- `rc` channel support
- `CHANGELOG.md` generation step (Keep-a-Changelog format from conventional commits)
- `NPM_TOKEN: ""` (defense-in-depth for OIDC)
- No more `--provenance` flag (automatic with OIDC)

**Confidence**: HIGH — every line is grounded in cited current docs. Only caveat: requires npmjs.com side configuration (next section).

input.content:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -E 'closes \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric 'closes [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Quality & Validation

# Runs comprehensive quality checks on all PRs:
# - Prettier (formatting)
# - ESLint (linting)
# - markdownlint (markdown quality)
# - Schema validation (YAML structure)
# - Agent schema tests (fixture-based validation)
# - Installation component tests (CLI + compilation)
# - Workflow state consistency tests
# - Knowledge base tests
# - Skill structure validation
# - File reference validation
# - Python script tests (pytest via uv)

"on":
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  prettier:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Prettier format check
        run: npm run format:check

  eslint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: ESLint
        run: npm run lint

  markdownlint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: markdownlint
        run: npm run lint:md

  validate:
    # Matrix Linux + Windows to catch platform-divergence bugs (path handling,
    # line endings, shell scripts). macOS skipped: POSIX-equivalent to Linux
    # for SKF's Node + Python surface area — add only if a Mac-only bug surfaces.
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Validate YAML schemas
        run: npm run validate:schemas

      - name: Run agent schema validation tests
        run: npm run test:schemas

      - name: Test installation components
        run: npm run test:install

      - name: Test CLI integration
        run: npm run test:cli

      - name: Test workflow state consistency
        run: npm run test:workflow

      - name: Test knowledge base
        run: npm run test:knowledge

      - name: Validate skills structure
        run: npm run validate:skills

      - name: Validate file references
        run: npm run validate:refs

  python:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run Python tests
        run: npm run test:python

lines: +      - name: Smoke test conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution (Windows path/EOL check)

input.description: Verify existing docs refs match literal release.yaml

content.content:
docs/RELEASING.md:42:**Bypass actors:** `RepositoryRole` actor_id=5 (Admin), `bypass_mode: pull_request`. Admins can bypass the ruleset **only via a pull request**, never via direct push. This preserves `non_fast_forward` and the required-checks gate for the `github-actions[bot]` account that the future `release.yaml` workflow (Story 3.1) will use to push tags and commits to `main`. **Do not add a bot-specific bypass**; it would defeat the whole purpose of this ruleset.
docs/RELEASING.md:87:**Coupling with npm trusted publishing (Story 1.3).** The npm trusted publisher binds on four fields — `organization=armelhbobdad`, `repository=bmad-module-skill-forge`, `workflow filename=release.yaml`, `environment=release`. The environment name above is load-bearing: any rename here must be accompanied by a matching npm-side update in the same change, or the next publish returns 404.
docs/RELEASING.md:157:The future `release.yaml` workflow (Story 3.1) publishes to npm via **OIDC trusted publishing** — no `NPM_TOKEN` is consulted during the publish step, and every published version carries an auto-attached SLSA Build Level 2 provenance attestation. For this to work, the npm package `bmad-module-skill-forge` has a trusted-publisher entry on npmjs.com that binds on four fields exactly matching what the workflow asserts at run time. A mismatch on any field causes an opaque `404` at publish time — the error ("npm could not match your workflow run") surfaces the failure class but does not name which of the four fields is wrong.
docs/RELEASING.md:166:| Workflow filename | `release.yaml`            |
docs/RELEASING.md:173:**Rename coupling — the four fields are load-bearing.** Renaming OR deleting the `release` GitHub environment (see § Release Environment), renaming or moving `release.yaml` within `.github/workflows/`, or flipping the extension between `.yaml` and `.yml` each require matching updates in the same PR to: (a) the npm-side Trusted Publisher, (b) the Registered table above, and (c) the `## Release Process` enumeration in `README.md` (which names both `release` and the Trusted Publisher). Skipping any of these produces an opaque `404` on the next publish — the error names the failure class, not the specific field.
docs/RELEASING.md:175:**Pre-registration inversion.** This entry was registered **before** `release.yaml` was authored (Story 3.1). The first live validator of the full OIDC chain is Story 3.2's alpha cut. If that cut's publish step 404s, open a **three-way comparison**: (1) the npm Settings tab, (2) the workflow YAML's `name` / `on` / `jobs.<id>.environment` lines, and (3) the Registered table above. The table is the ground truth because it captured the values at npm-save time — compare both the npm record and the workflow header against the table, never the workflow against itself (verifying the workflow against its own header will silently confirm a typo).
docs/RELEASING.md:177:**`NPM_TOKEN` is a legacy residual, not a safety net.** The token remains at repo-level secrets for two reasons: (a) the legacy `publish.yaml` still uses it until Story 3.3 retires that path, and (b) Story 6.3 deletes the secret entirely post-v1.0.0. Treat its continued presence as attack surface to minimize, not defence-in-depth — a repo-scope token is reachable from any workflow with `secrets.*` access. The token does NOT sit "behind" OIDC: `release.yaml` does not yet exist, so there is no OIDC path for it to be a fallback to. If a future OIDC incident forces a last-resort token-based re-publish, document the flip in the commit body and revert as soon as OIDC is restored.
docs/RELEASING.md:179:**Fixing a bad registration.** The npm UI exposes both **Edit** and **Delete** on an existing Trusted Publisher entry (observed 2026-04-20). Prefer edit for a single-field typo; prefer delete-and-re-add if multiple fields are wrong or the edit form ever feels ambiguous. **Pre-Story 3.2**: there is no destructive side effect because no publish is attempted yet, and delete-and-re-add keeps the audit trail cleaner. **Post-Story 3.2**: a publish that fires during the delete-and-re-add window will 404 — gate any delete-and-re-add behind a manual publish freeze (pause any active `release.yaml` runs, confirm no tags are in-flight) before touching the entry.
